task_completion.description: Open the 'airline_profitability.xlsx' file and navigate to the 'Route_Financials' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Excel application is open with 'airline_profitability.xlsx' in the title bar, and the 'Route_Financials' tab is active at the bottom.

task_completion.description: Determine Seats, Available Seat Miles (ASM), and Revenue Passenger Miles (RPM) for the routes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Columns for Seats, ASM, and RPM (likely columns F, G, H) are populated with numerical values for the 15 route rows. The formula bar may show references to the 'Fleet_Specs' sheet for Seats.

task_completion.description: Calculate the Load Factor and format it as a percentage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Load Factor column (likely column I) is populated with numerical values that include a '%' sign (e.g., 85%).

task_completion.description: Calculate Total Revenue and Total Cost using the provided formulas (including flight duration based on Distance and Average Block Speed).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Total Revenue and Total Cost columns (likely columns J and K) are populated with numerical values. The formula bar for Total Cost should show a calculation involving division (for duration) and multiplication (by hourly cost).

task_completion.description: Record the Operating Margin for each route.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Operating Margin column (likely column L) is populated with numerical values, showing a mix of positive and negative numbers.

task_completion.description: Set the Profitability status to 'REVIEW' for negative margins and 'OK' for all others.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Profitability column (likely column M) is populated with the text 'REVIEW' or 'OK'. There should be between 3 and 6 'REVIEW' entries visible.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the 'airline_profitability.xlsx' file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the save icon (floppy disk), uses a save shortcut (Ctrl+S), or the title bar indicates the file is saved (no unsaved dot/asterisk).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the spreadsheet application's GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Excel or a similar spreadsheet application being actively used, rather than a terminal, Python script, or text editor modifying the file.

integrity.description: The agent used spreadsheet formulas to calculate the values rather than hardcoding the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: When cells are selected, the formula bar shows actual formulas (e.g., VLOOKUP, IF, arithmetic operations) instead of static typed numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy-paste a pre-computed table of answers directly into the sheet.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen building the columns step-by-step or dragging down formulas, rather than pasting a massive block of final text/values from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'airline_profitability.xlsx' containing 'Route_Financials' and 'Fleet_Specs' sheets. The 'Route_Financials' sheet has exactly 15 rows of operational data. When correctly calculated, between 3 and 6 routes will have a negative operating margin and must be marked as 'REVIEW' in the Profitability column, while the rest should be marked 'OK'.
